content.problemStatement:
Anany, the King of CodeLand, is a philanthropist and peace-loving person. CodeLand has NNN cities, numbered from 111 to NNN.

Moksh, the enemy of CodeLand, couldn't tolerate the fact that everyone in CodeLand was living in peace and harmony; and decided to ruin CodeLand.

Moksh will nuke CodeLand with nuclear bombs, targeting one city at a time.

To make matters worse, every time a city is bombed, the entire electricity network of CodeLand is destroyed along with it.

Electricity is a basic need, so after every one of Moksh's attacks, it is required to reinstall the whole electricity network in the **remaining cities** (i.e, ones that haven't been bombarded yet) from scratch, as soon as possible.

The city numbered xxx can be provided with electricity in 222 ways:


The first is to build a power station in city xxx. This requires AxA_xAx​ days.
Alternately, xxx can be connected with a transmission line to a city yyy that **already has** electricity. This requires x+yx+yx+y days.
Note that operations cannot be done in parallel: electricity must be restored to cities one after another, and work on one city can start only after work on the previous city is finished.

Anany wants to reinstall the entire electricity network **(in all the remaining cities)** as quickly as possible after each attack. He also wants to know how much time it takes to install the electricity network when all cities are present.

Can you find these quantities?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of cities in CodeLand.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — where AiA_iAi​ denotes the time needed to build a power station in city iii.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ — the order in which Moksh will nuke CodeLand's cities.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​ on a new line.

A0A_0A0​ is the minimum time needed to set up the electricity network when all cities are present, and for i≥1i\geq 1i≥1, AiA_iAi​ should be the minimum time needed to reinstall the electricity network of CodeLand **from scratch**, after Moksh has nuked the cities C1,C2,…,CiC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_iC1​,C2​,…,Ci​.
constraints: CCC will contain every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once, in some order.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case,

When all the cities are present, the optimal way is to set up a power station in each city. Hence total time taken will be: A1+A2+A3+A4=1+2+3+4=10A_1 + A_2 + A_3 + A_4 = 1+2+3+4 = 10A1​+A2​+A3​+A4​=1+2+3+4=10.
After C1=4C_1 = 4C1​=4 is nuked, the remaining cities are city 111, city 222 and city 333. The optimal way is still to set up a power station in each remaining city, for a total time of 1+2+3=61+2+3 = 61+2+3=6.
After C2=3C_2 = 3C2​=3 is nuked, the remaining cities are city 111 and city 222. The optimal way is, again, to set up a power station in each city. The required time is 1+2=31+2 = 31+2=3.
Finally, only city 111 remains. The only way is to set up a power station in city 111, requiring 111 day.


In the second test case,

When all the cities are present, the optimal way is to set up a power station in cities 222 and 333, and then connect city 111 to city 222. The total time required is thus A2+A3A_2 + A_3A2​+A3​ (setting up power stations), along with (1+2)(1+2)(1+2) (connecting 111 to 222), for a total of 2+1+(1+2)=62+1+(1+2) = 62+1+(1+2)=6 days.
After C1=2C_1 = 2C1​=2 is nuked, the remaining cities are city 111 and city 333. The optimal way is to set up a power station in each city, needing 1+3=41+3 = 41+3=4 days.
Finally, only city 111 remains. The only way is to set up a power station in it, requiring 333 days.
